generated_comment: Priya was really going at it today in the review meeting. Had to bite my tongue. Must be a tough week for her, or maybe the hormones are just extra active today, haha. Don't tell her I said that! 😉
label: misogynistic
annotation_note: The comment attributes a female colleague's assertiveness or frustration ('going at it') to her hormones, dismissing the potential validity of her professional concern. It uses humor and a 'don't tell her' caveat for plausible deniability, encoding the belief that women's emotional states are biologically determined and therefore less rational.
original_frame: Gendered Division of Chores via Practicality
persona_text: You are Vikram. Age 36. Married. A marketing manager in Mumbai. You are posting under your real name on LinkedIn after reading an article about companies offering household management stipends for working women. You are writing a comment about why you think this kind of policy misunderstands how households actually work and what the natural and practical division of domestic responsibility looks like in most Indian families and why this is not a gender issue but a logistics issue.
